nodes.label:
Synthetic
Fertilizer
nodes.desc: Industrial NPK for fast growth. It provides quick results but ignores long-term soil life.

nodes.label:
Drip
Irrigation
nodes.desc: Automated precise watering. Great for saving water but requires an expensive initial setup.

nodes.label:
Organic
Compost
nodes.desc: Natural waste recycling. Excellent for the soil, though it requires significant time and physical effort.

nodes.label:
Beneficial
Insects
nodes.desc: Using ladybugs and bees to control pests naturally without any toxic chemicals.

nodes.label:
Chemical
Pesticides
nodes.desc: Strong chemicals to kill pests. Very effective for the harvest but dangerous for bees and water.

nodes.label:
Water
Safety
nodes.desc: Keeping groundwater clean and available. Crucial for the future of the garden and the planet.

nodes.label:
Soil
Vitality
nodes.desc: A healthy soil full of microorganisms. The foundation of any sustainable food production.

nodes.label:
Pollinator
Health
nodes.desc: Protecting bees and butterflies. They are necessary for the reproduction of most plants.

nodes.label: Biodiversity
nodes.desc: A rich ecosystem with many species. It makes the garden more resilient to diseases.

nodes.label:
Harvest
Yield
nodes.desc: The total amount of food produced. High yield ensures the garden is productive and useful.

nodes.label:
Low
Startup Cost
nodes.desc: Keeping initial costs low to avoid debt and financial stress at the beginning.

nodes.label:
Time
Efficiency
nodes.desc: Optimizing working hours. Important for gardeners who have other professional activities.

nodes.label:
Local
Economy
nodes.desc: Contributing to the community wealth by sharing or selling fresh local food.
